item.title: Can we...?
item.description: A dog who will listen reliably is WAY more fun to have around, and it’s so much easier to involve them in all the activities you want to without stress or frustration.
item.dc:creator: Iullyiullyus

item.title: Updated meme format for "Change My Mind"
item.dc:creator: psuedon

item.title: The Saga of Sam
item.description:
Everyone - meet Sam. Again.
Sam was my foster in December before getting adopted, he's amazing The adopters had a cat that was terrorizing Sam and he was returned to the rescue
I've taken in dozens of high anxiety and reactive dogs but when Sam came back it's the first time I'd describe a dog as simply - Sad. I think he knew as soon as his family dropped him off that he'd never see them again.
I took him out for runs, gave him every treat he wanted, I did everything I could But for the first few days Sam didn't want to be touched and spent every second inside his kennel.
This is not the Sam I remember. He was scared of everything, wanted nothing to do with my dogs and whined at the top of his lungs He had a rough life before he got to the rescue, it looked like getting adopted and returned stole the rest of his hope.
We kept up with the runs every single day, his whining got a little less as the days passed He started trusting me a little bit more every run
He started bonding with my dogs and taking cues from them My 2 have ran 9,000 miles they've seen it all, Sam started to learn that if they're not scared - maybe he doesn't need to be either
After a couple days he started curling up in bed and following my every step As cute as it was, I couldn't help but wonder if he was just thinking "please don't leave me" the entire time
On the 12th day, yesterday, everything changed Look at his strong and confident body language, it was the first big run that he didn't whine at all
IT WAS LIKE A BRAND NEW SAMUEL!!! Somewhere around the 40th mile that he ran with us he finally broke out of that sadness
Downtown, next to trains, busses, sirens, traffic, none of it bothered him Sam was getting his confidence back and it was so incredibly beautiful to watch.
Ok so he was still a little afraid of water but PJ has his back
I usually like to make these posts when I have good news that the dog is adopted But today is not that post, Sam is still with me and he's doing amazing
I wanted to make this post because maybe Sam has a potential adopter out there reading this right now
Sam is a 5 year old 25lb beagle and he's the most cuddly and affectionate dog I've ever fostered He's great with all dogs, perfect on a leash and given a little time to trust - he loves all humans too
Sam is up for adoption in the Minneapolis/St Paul area and he's going to make some person or family incredibly happy and well-cuddled for the rest of his life He deserves incredible things because he's an amazing dog and so full of love https://healingheartsrescue.org/
Can't adopt Sam but still want to help? I entered him in the rescue's pet photo contest
You can donate as little as $1 in Sam's name to help the rescue I volunteer for continue saving dogs like him If he's one of the top 12 dogs in the contest he wins a professional photography shoot that'll only increase his odds at finding a wonderful home
I usually don't get emotionally attached to fosters, it just makes saying goodbye even tougher With Sam it's hard not to feel for a dog whose been through so much when all he wants is a stable home and endless cuddles.
I love you Samuel, you've got a perfect adopter out there somewhere and I promise you I will find them. ... If you'd like to help the rescue league in Sam's honor you can see his page here - https://www.gogophotocontest.com/healinghearts/entries/572689 For a volunteer based rescue every small donation goes so far in helping dogs like Sam all over the country ... Hopefully soon you'll see another post in all caps with a picture of Sam with his adoptive family.

item.title: Don’t Use Fake info to Sign Up To Volunteer for the Republicans
item.description: Don’t use this link! : https://gop.com/volunteer/ Register to vote: https://vote.gov —————— Get Involved: Donate to a good voter registration org: https://bsky.app/profile/fieldteam6.bsky.social —————— Contact your reps: Senate: https://www.senate.gov/senators/senators-contact.htm?Class=1 House of Representatives: https://contactrepresentatives.org/

item.title: Currently (Re)reading
item.description: (Re)reading one of my favorites. For every copy of the Gideon's Bible out there, I wish I could to put a copy of The Demon-Haunted World in hotels, hospitals, clinics, schools, etc. Everyone should read this book.
item.dc:creator: evolution4407

item.title: A museum bought my drawing!
item.description:
Almost a year ago I shared a post about my cash register drawing that ended up going viral here and even attracting the attention of @ElbowDeepInAHorse A bunch of you lovely people reached out to inquire about the original and I'm beyond humbled that The Fort Wayne museum has just purchased my drawing! I'm so excited to celebrate the incredible design of this beautiful object and to bring more attention to the makers of the National Cash Register and their incredible artistry!
Thank you so much for looking! I have other finished drawings and timelapse you can see at www.instagram.com/e.copeland
item.dc:creator: emilycopelandartistry

item.title: What Happened Today #69
item.description: Trump isn’t just rewriting the rules. He’s coming for your rights. He tried to cut off Social Security phone lines. He handed your tax data to Musk’s private army. He’s punishing lawyers who held Fox accountable for its 2020 election lies by winning a $787 million settlement. In your state. At the agency helping your family. Inside your benefits. None of this is theoretical. It’s personal. The courts are blinking. Congress is buckling. And he’s betting you won’t notice until it’s too late. Call your reps. Tell them we've all seen the playbook. Tell them that you won’t stay quiet while your future gets sold off to cronies and cults. Then get out there and prove it. Subscribe free at https://govbrief.today #GovBriefToday #Resist 1. https://www.axios.com/2025/04/09/trump-tariffs-pause-china-stocks-recession 2. https://time.com/7276234/trump-tariff-insider-trading-schiff/ 3. https://apnews.com/article/trump-deportations-alien-enemies-act-5eb0e061b4cc0d929f0050d7adb9b133 4. https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2025/apr/09/kash-patel-director-atf-bureau-removal 5. https://www.msn.com/en-ca/money/topstories/fda-reverses-course-on-telework-after-layoffs-and-resignations-threaten-basic-operations/ar-AA1CCHLs 6. https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/elections/disaster-gretchen-whitmers-talk-tariffs-meeting-trump-anger-fellow-dem-rcna200530 7. https://www.msn.com/en-gb/news/world/trump-repeats-threat-to-use-military-force-if-iran-does-not-agree-to-nuclear-deal/ar-AA1CCKA7 8. https://www.politico.com/news/2025/04/09/mike-huckabee-israel-ambassador-032907 9. https://www.reviewjournal.com/news/nation-and-world/suspected-us-strikes-in-yemen-kill-at-least-10-houthi-terrorists-say-3349235/ 10. https://thehill.com/policy/energy-environment/5241575-trump-orders-his-administration-to-repeal-biden-shower-head-flow-restrictions/ 11. https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/politics/2025/04/09/trump-orders-probe-krebs-2020-election/83016002007/ 12. https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2025/apr/09/trump-executive-order-fox-news-law-firm 13. https://abcnews.go.com/Politics/dhs-screen-social-media-visa-applicants-antisemitic-activity/story?id=120642944 14. https://www.chicagotribune.com/2025/04/09/bank-overdraft-fees/ 15. https://www.hrdive.com/news/fired-eeoc-commissioner-samuels-files-lawsuit-against-trump/744876/ 16. https://www.newsweek.com/social-security-halts-change-days-before-scheduled-start-what-know-2057674 17. https://www.wired.com/story/gao-audit-elon-musk-doge-government-agencies/ 18. https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2025-04-09/trump-signs-order-to-set-up-port-fees-doge-review-of-navy 19. https://www.masslive.com/politics/2025/04/mass-schools-boss-defies-trump-dei-edict-state-will-continue-to-promote-diversity.html Back reading comments later today or tomorrow. Work got a little busier than expected!
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.title: Holy christ. The videos are bad. Very bad.
item.description: 9 bullets in the teenager. Leg amputated. Critical condition. No idea if he's brain-dead or not, they're going to run some brain-activity tests this week. And Pocatello PD is trying to downplay their fuck-up. They had a chain-link fence separating the officers from the teenager, and the teenager was being restrained by the t-shirt by someone else (not a cop). They did not even attempt to get information from the locals, or try to understand the situation. Almost as if they saw him as a target and not as a person. https://apnews.com/article/idaho-police-autism-shooting-teen-d9eef615233c7e95d4fa3ee8a627f7d2 ACAB, indeed.
item.dc:creator: NKato
